agency: New York City Office of the Comptroller
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting). Two properties: comptroller.nyc.gov (WordPress — wp-json, wp-sitemap custom types report/rfp/event/nyc_bonds/job; 67 /services/ pages) and checkbooknyc.com (Drupal). The existing Checkbook NYC API (POST /api, XML) was probed LIVE across its type_of_data domains. Open Data reconciled via the Socrata Discovery API using verified agency label 'Office of the Comptroller (COMPTROLLER)' (15 assets).
headline_findings: The Comptroller ALREADY runs a public API — Checkbook NYC (POST checkbooknyc.com/api) — covering Spending, Contracts, Budget, Revenue, and Payroll; probed live at 3.2M spending / 10.2M payroll / 1.2M revenue FY2024 records, no key required.
But it is XML request/response, POST-only, with a bespoke search_criteria/response_columns envelope — no OpenAPI, no JSON Schema, no MCP, not resource-oriented, not agent-native.

The office's data is split across THREE surfaces: Checkbook (spending/contracts), NYC Open Data (15 assets — pension holdings x5, claims report, published audits, bonds, debt service), and the WordPress site (audits/RFPs/eClaim).
checkbooknyc.com is a Drupal app; comptroller.nyc.gov is WordPress; both sit behind Imperva. Comptroller Mark Levine per site metadata.
The one citizen write-workflow with no API is filing a claim against the City (eClaim / file-a-claim) — the net-new write object ClaimFiling.
See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md for the API + vendor inventory.

apis_observed.note: EXISTING public spending API. type_of_data = Spending/Contracts/Budget/Revenue/Payroll. Probed live (Spending 3.2M FY24). XML envelope, POST-only, no OpenAPI/JSON/MCP, vendor Drupal — not agent-native.
open; content (reports, RFPs, events, nyc_bonds), not financial resources; FacetWP search.
15 Comptroller assets — pension holdings (5 systems), claims report, published audits, bonds, debt service; flattened snapshots; top asset is an href to Checkbook.
site search/filtering; not a data API.

fruit.name: City Spending (payments)
fruit.notes: 3,227,575 FY2024 records via POST /api type_of_data=Spending. Real API, XML envelope — not resource-oriented/JSON/agent-native.

fruit.name: City Contracts
fruit.notes: type_of_data=Contracts (requires status + category). The Comptroller registers all City contracts; a late-contracts dashboard exists on the site. No Socrata twin.

fruit.name: Budget / Revenue / Payroll
fruit.notes: type_of_data Budget/Revenue/Payroll all reachable; Payroll 10.2M, Revenue 1.2M FY24 records. API-only, XML.

fruit.name: Published Audits
fruit.notes: Bureau of Audit reports; published-audit list on Open Data, reports as WordPress custom posts.

fruit.name: Claims Against the City (reported)
opendata_match: Claims Report - Underlying Settlements and Claims Filed Data ex6k-ym48 (10c)
fruit.notes: Reported claim dispositions by agency/borough/fiscal year. Read/report view; the filing view has no API.

fruit.name: Pension Fund Holdings
fruit.notes: Comptroller is investment adviser/custodian for the five NYC pension systems; per-security holdings on Open Data.

fruit.name: Bonds & Debt Service
opendata_match: General Obligation Debt Service by Fiscal Year dfr8-nudu (4c)
GO Variable Rate Bonds Credit and Liquidity Agreements xprg-trsi (7c)
fruit.notes: Bond program tracked as nyc_bonds WordPress posts plus debt-service datasets. (Modeled loosely under Audit/reporting.)

fruit.name: File a Claim Against the City
fruit.notes: The Comptroller's eClaim system lets residents file a claim (injury/property/vehicle) against the City. A real citizen transaction with no API. The one net-new write surface for this domain.
